reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 run in a Web Worker: fresh profile → Download (~29 MB) → auto-init ready → Classify on the cats sample returned tabby 67.9%, tiger cat 20.9%, Egyptian cat 1.0% (correct ImageNet labels), 226 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Pre-verified in Node too: convnext-tiny q8 on the HF tiger.jpg → tiger 0.74 via both the pipeline and the low-level processor→model path. conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w17bshots/convnext-classification-{light,dark,mobile}.png
rubric.notes: Distinct architecture from the ViT demo (same task): ConvNeXt is a pure ConvNet, no attention/patches.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output.logits is [1,1000]; worker computes softmax + top-k + entropy + top-1 margin. Semantic sanity holds: the cats photo yields cat classes ranked top, the See-inside table shows logit next to softmax %, and the head-to-head against ViT on the same image reports agreement/disagreement with each model's top-1. conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: 1000 classes — one fewer than MobileNet's 1001 (no background class).

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/convnext-tiny-224 linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker in the shared classify-ui.js ClassifierEngine). At-a-glance also states params (~28M), 7×7 depthwise convs, Apache-2.0.

Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~29 MB)'; clicking streams progress and the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready'. Cached reload → state=ready with NO Download button (only 'Clear cached model'); all four sub-pages (basics/practical/wild/multimodel) auto-init from the session cache to ready with 0 console errors. conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Verified across the state matrix: first visit + current-cached + sub-page reuse.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — streamed download progress; Retry surfaced by the shared loader on failure; classification errors go to a .status.err region ('Classification failed: …'); the head-to-head loads ViT lazily with its own progress line and honest ~88 MB note. Runs on the WASM path with no GPU (headless no-GPU produced real labels). conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.

Agent verdict — Classify button, top-k slider (live label), keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails (Enter/Space), 'Compare on this image' head-to-head button, the conv-vs-transformer <details>, and per-sub-page controls (batch tag slider + tag-cloud filter chips; the wild transform toggle chips + rotate slider; the ensemble button) all operable. conformance: controls-operable=manual.

Agent verdict — every label comes from the on-device worker run (verified real output tabby 67.9% / tiger cat 20.9%). The head-to-head and ensemble reuse the same real ClassifierEngine driving the ConvNeXt and ViT workers; no canned result anywhere. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + run status; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; sample thumbnails keyboard-operable; transform toggles set aria-pressed; chips have min-block-size 40px.

Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 (dark) and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both render with no horizontal overflow; the at-a-glance table and versus-grid wrap (versus-grid is grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so the two model columns stack on narrow screens); bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere. responsive-check programmatic pass on both classes; screenshots reviewed. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual.

Agent verdict — all inference (processor + model forward) runs in a Web Worker via the shared ClassifierEngine; the control UI (sliders, toggles) never blocks on inference; the head-to-head runs ConvNeXt + ViT with Promise.all off-thread. Main-thread eval stayed responsive during load; 0 console errors. conformance: inp-stays-low=manual.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two-column head-to-head comparison layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox/grid with intrinsic sizing + flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap on items with long unbreakable content; repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for responsive card grids.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the ConvNeXt-vs-ViT panels use .versus-grid = grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so they sit side-by-side on desktop and stack on mobile with no overflow; versus-col has min-inline-size:0; bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere; the run panel uses flex:1 1 280px with flex-wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; screenshots w17bshots/convnext-classification-{dark,mobile}.png show no horizontal overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live status region loading progress and keyboard-operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a role=status aria-live region; adequate tap targets; keyboard operability for custom controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + run status are role=status aria-live=polite; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow; sample thumbnails and dropzone handle Enter/Space; transform toggles expose aria-pressed; chips have min-block-size 40px.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep inference off the main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy/background work off the main thread and pause it when off-screen.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all classification runs in a Web Worker (ClassifierEngine); the head-to-head/ensemble run both workers concurrently off-thread, keeping the control UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; UI responsive during the 226 ms WASM run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; the page links it.

openQuestions: Could a WebGPU execution-provider path (honest WASM fallback retained) speed ConvNeXt up on capable devices and make the ViT head-to-head snappier?
Is q8 ConvNeXt-Tiny meaningfully less accurate than fp32 on hard images, and worth surfacing in the head-to-head?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a ConvNeXt-vs-ViT texture-vs-shape side-by-side: apply the same transform to one image and show both architectures' confidence collapse together (probes the ConvNet texture-bias vs transformer shape-bias claim directly).
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for convnext-classification with an honest WASM fallback.

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for convnext-classification: a NEW image-classification demo for a DISTINCT architecture (pure ConvNet) on the same ImageNet task as the built ViT. Real ~29 MB download + on-device transformers.js q8 run verified in headless Chrome (cats sample → tabby 67.9%, tiger cat 20.9%, 226 ms WASM, 1000 classes). The ConvNeXt-vs-ViT head-to-head loads ViT lazily and reports agreement/disagreement + per-model latency, making the ConvNet-vs-transformer contrast legible. Cached reload auto-inits with no Download button; all 4 sub-pages (basics/practical batch-tagging/wild texture-vs-shape/multimodel ensemble) auto-init clean. Light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (no overflow, legible, ≥40px controls). 23 immutable conformance assertions authored; both gates green.
